steps.title: Find the multiplicative factor when w increases by 1
steps.content:
When $w$ increases by 1, the exponent $3w$ increases by 3:
$$z(w+1) = (0.835)^{3(w+1)} = (0.835)^{3w} \cdot (0.835)^3 = z(w) \cdot (0.835)^3$$
So each unit increase in $w$ multiplies $z$ by $(0.835)^3$.

steps.title: Compute $(0.835)^3$
steps.content:
$(0.835)^2 = 0.697225$
$(0.835)^3 = 0.697225 \times 0.835 \approx 0.5822$

The new value is about 58.22% of the previous value.

steps.title: Convert to a percentage decrease
steps.content:
A decrease of $p$% means the new value $= (1 - p/100) \times$ old value.
$$1 - \frac{p}{100} = 0.5822$$
$$\frac{p}{100} = 0.4178$$
$$p \approx 41.8$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: 41.8 — Matches our calculation: the function decreases by approximately 41.8% per unit increase in $w$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: 16.5 — This would correspond to a per-step factor of $1 - 0.165 = 0.835$, which is the base of the exponent, not the per-unit-$w$ factor. This ignores the coefficient 3 in the exponent. Eliminated.
  • C: 0.835 — This is the base itself, not a percentage. Eliminated.
  • D: 0.165 — This is $1 - 0.835$, which would be the percentage decrease per unit increase in the exponent, not per unit increase in $w$. Eliminated.
